titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in 377
GA 377
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Transcription changed at 18.24, 28 by David Parker.
τη deleted at 18.20 by David Parker.
κεδρων changed to κενδρων by David Parker.
Repeat verse marker at 13.8 added by David Parker.
The following page numbers changed by Rachel Kevern: P271v to P270v, P272r to P271r, P271av to P271v, P272ar to P272r.
ην ανδρεας changed to ην δε ανδρεας at 1.40 and και changed to κατα at 2.23; a word in 5.4 moved to a different line; line numbers added at P270r and P270v by Rachel Kevern.
Word breaks in 13.9 and 17.25 corrected by David Parker.
Adjusted to XML specifications 1.4 by Catherine Smith.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0.

body.note: Status:G-A Number: 303770Name of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 267rBase text used: TRns Transcribed by: Eirini PanouTranscription begun: September 2011Transcription finished: October 2011 Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate): Lectionary annotations - Yes Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No Other remarks:

ab.note: The scribe wrote o then replaced it with iota before accenting it so this is treated as a modification in scribendo
